chief_complaint: A man in his 50s presented with loss of appetite, diarrhoea, chills, and general malaise.
history: The patient had consciousness disturbance, thyrotoxicosis, and thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) suppression. He was diagnosed with thyroid storm caused by subacute thyroiditis and treated with prednisolone and propranolol.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: Elevated thyroglobulin and C reactive protein levels, suppressed TSH, and inverted T waves on ECG. A large thrombus was detected in the left ventricle.
correct_diagnosis: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with left ventricle thrombus caused by subacute thyroiditis
differentials: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with left ventricle thrombus caused by subacute thyroiditis
correct_ranking: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy with left ventricle thrombus caused by subacute thyroiditis
